codeReview.summary: Implementation is close, but two blocking correctness issues remain: renewal does not preserve original TTL after the first renewal for non-default leases, and active-lease exclusivity is enforced with a non-atomic check-then-insert pattern that does not guarantee exactly-one success under real concurrency.

issues.description: `renewLease` does not preserve the original TTL once a lease has already been renewed. `getOriginalTtlMs` returns `DEFAULT_LEASE_TTL_SEC` whenever `renewedAt` is set, so a lease granted with custom TTL (e.g. checkout TTL 60s) gets renewed to 300s on second+ renewal, violating the expected behavior to extend by the original TTL.

`grantLease` enforces one-active-lease via `SELECT` then `INSERT` without transactional locking or a DB uniqueness guard. Concurrent callers can pass the pre-check before either insert commits, allowing multiple active leases for the same issue and violating the required concurrent behavior (exactly one succeeds).
Renewal tests do not cover repeated renewals for custom TTL leases; they only assert monotonic extension for default TTL. This gap allowed the TTL-regression bug to pass tests.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `backend-worker` procedure specifies a red phase (`Run pnpm test:run — new tests should FAIL`) before implementation, but the transcript shows tests were created and implementation was added before any lease-manager test execution. `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true` in handoff appears overly permissive.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/backend-worker/SKILL.md:32-43; transcript skeleton for session 0375e79d-3cd8-4518-9fa2-b162e060de4f shows Create lease-manager.test.ts -> Create lease-manager.ts -> Execute `npx vitest run src/__tests__/lease-manager.test.ts`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Shared command guidance is inconsistent for test concurrency: `services.yaml` declares `pnpm test:run --max-workers=1`, but worker ran full-suite tests with `--max-workers=4`.

summary: Reviewed handoff, diff, transcript skeleton, and skill/doc context for lease-manager-service. Result: fail due to two blocking issues (non-atomic concurrent grant enforcement and incorrect TTL preservation across repeated renewals for custom TTL leases), plus one test-coverage gap and two shared-state observations.
